consistency.naming: Consistent. 'Shillelagh' is a known magical item/concept.
consistency.mechanics_description: Clear and internally consistent. It modifies attack rolls, damage rolls, and damage die size for a specific weapon.
consistency.components: Components (V, S, M) are standard and appropriately detailed for a low-level transmutation.

quality.flavor: High flavor for a low-level Druid spell, referencing traditional Celtic magic.
quality.clarity: High clarity. The rules for use, ending conditions (casting again or letting go), and effect duration are explicitly stated.
quality.balance: Appropriate for Level 0 (Cantrip). Providing an attack stat change (using Spellcasting Ability over Strength) and a small damage increase (d6 to d8) is solid utility for a caster who might otherwise have poor melee stats.

mechanics.casting_time: 1 bonus action (Very efficient for setting up combat).
mechanics.duration: 1 minute (Standard for combat cantrips, suggesting multiple rounds of engagement).
effects_summary: Melee attacks use Spellcasting Ability modifier instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls.
Damage die increases to a d8.
Ends if recast or weapon is dropped.
mechanics.potential_issues: None identified. Mechanics are well-defined and standard for D&D 5e spell design conventions.

data_validation.schema_adherence: Valid structure for spell entity analysis.
data_validation.data_completeness: All required fields from RAW DATA are present and analyzed.
